name: Implement a first-class supervisor agent with persistent toolbar visibility

rounds.prompt: Implement a first-class workflow monitor agent that serves the supervisory role currently requiring a human or external Codex session while Refine agents are active. The monitor must run outside the UI request path and remain active whenever Goal workflow work is active or queued. It should observe Goal transitions, agent/process health, runtime-worker reachability, Git/state synchronization, worktree/branch publication and merge progress, and UI/daemon health signals; detect actionable stalls, crashes, interrupted agents, provider failures, merge conflicts, and state-sync failures; apply only bounded, safe, auditable recoveries automatically; and surface durable, actionable progress and errors in the UI so a user can understand what happened and retry. It must never freeze the UI, must not disguise provider/auth failures, must avoid destructive or unbounded repair, and must preserve the existing rule that an interrupted Goal enters error/failed state and can be restarted later. Provide settings and lifecycle behavior that avoid duplicate monitors across restarts, make recovery idempotent, and stop or idle cleanly when no workflow work remains. Reuse shared backend services so CLI/API/UI can observe the same monitor state. Include proportional unit and integration coverage for active-work lifecycle, worker loss/restart, stalled work, safe recovery, unrecoverable failure, daemon restart/reconnect, concurrency, and no-work idle behavior, plus operator/user documentation and an intent/architecture update explaining the recovery boundary.

rounds.implementation_report:
Ready for review at commit `3a6d59d`; the worktree is clean. Refine will advance the Goal from `in-progress` after this agent handoff.

After-action report:

- Changed: Added a durable supervisor agent, shared CLI/API state, interactive persistent toolbar chat, bounded recovery tracking, settings, tests, and documentation.
- Why: Refine can supervise active workflows without external babysitting while preserving safe recovery and audit boundaries.
- Verification:
  - `cargo test` — exit 0; 370 passed, 0 failed.
  - `cargo check --all-targets` — exit 0.
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — exit 0.
  - `cargo fmt --all -- --check` — exit 0.
  - JavaScript syntax checks — exit 0.
  - `git diff --cached --check` — exit 0.
  - `xtask test-cli` — exit 1 on both runs at the pre-existing `cluster sync` assertion (`tests/cli_surface.rs:852`); it returned a running operation instead of `ok: true`, before reaching the supervisor CLI assertion.

Goal-agent elapsed time: approximately 27 minutes 39 seconds.
rounds.prompt:
Implement a first-class supervisor agent that serves the supervisory role currently requiring a human or external Codex session while Refine agents are active. Use “supervisor agent” consistently in product language, code-facing concepts, documentation, API fields, and the UI because it maps to Refine’s existing supervisor process model. Do not name this capability the “monitor agent.”

The supervisor agent must run outside the UI request path and remain active whenever Goal workflow work is active or queued. It should observe Goal transitions, agent/process health, runtime-worker reachability, Git/state synchronization, worktree/branch publication and merge progress, and UI/daemon health signals; detect actionable stalls, crashes, interrupted agents, provider failures, merge conflicts, and state-sync failures; apply only bounded, safe, auditable recoveries automatically; and expose durable, actionable state so users and other surfaces can understand what happened and retry. It must never freeze the UI, must not disguise provider/auth failures, must avoid destructive or unbounded repair, and must preserve the rule that an interrupted Goal enters error/failed state and can be restarted later. Provide settings and lifecycle behavior that avoid duplicate supervisor agents across restarts, make recovery idempotent, and stop or idle cleanly when no workflow work remains.

Add a persistent Supervisor tab to the existing web toolbar. The tab must remain discoverable even while the supervisor agent is idle and show live observations, current health/status, decisions, recovery attempts, outcomes, and actionable failures. It must reconnect safely after UI or daemon restart and must not depend on a Goal detail page being open. Implement the capability in shared backend services and durable state so UI, CLI, and API expose the same supervisor truth; keep the toolbar as a thin adapter.

Include proportional unit and integration coverage for active-work lifecycle, worker loss/restart, stalled work, safe recovery, unrecoverable failure, daemon restart/reconnect, concurrent Goals, duplicate-supervisor prevention, no-work idle behavior, toolbar persistence/live updates, and shared CLI/API/UI state, plus operator/user documentation and an intent/architecture update explaining the recovery boundary and relationship between the supervisor process and supervisor agent.

Make the persistent Supervisor toolbar tab interactive. The supervisor agent must accept user prompts and follow-up steering in the same way this Codex interface does. Reuse Refine’s existing chat session, message queueing, streaming output, reconnection, transcript, cancellation, and provider-error UI/backend capabilities instead of creating a separate conversation stack. The tab should combine conversational messages with structured supervisor health and recovery events without confusing the two, preserve the supervisor session across navigation and UI reconnects, allow prompts while workflows are active or idle, and clearly show when a request is queued, running, completed, failed, or blocked on provider/auth access. User prompts may guide investigation and bounded recovery but must not silently expand destructive authority; existing confirmation and audit rules still apply. Add tests for initial prompts, follow-ups during active work, queued input, reconnect/transcript restoration, provider failure, idle prompting, and coexistence with normal standalone and Goal chat tabs.

failed_actions.message: Supervisor turns launch directly through chat before WorkflowEngine evaluation but are not represented in WorkflowClaim concurrency accounting. A parallel_run_cap or per-provider cap of 1 can therefore still run the supervisor and Goal agent concurrently. The supervisor-session API also permits overriding the configured agent_cli provider.
failed_actions.rule: Agent roles must use the existing configured provider and concurrency services.

failed_actions.message: Tests verify singleton reconciliation, but none exercise a supervisor turn alongside WorkflowEngine under configured global or per-provider caps, leaving the concurrency-policy bypass untested.
failed_actions.rule: Concurrency changes require corresponding integration evidence.

rounds.prompt:
Implement a first-class supervisor agent that serves the supervisory role currently requiring a human or external Codex session while Refine agents are active. Use “supervisor agent” consistently in product language, code-facing concepts, documentation, API fields, and the UI because it maps to Refine’s existing supervisor process model. Do not name this capability the “monitor agent.”

The supervisor agent must run outside the UI request path and remain active whenever Goal workflow work is active or queued. It should observe Goal transitions, agent/process health, runtime-worker reachability, Git/state synchronization, worktree/branch publication and merge progress, and UI/daemon health signals; detect actionable stalls, crashes, interrupted agents, provider failures, merge conflicts, and state-sync failures; apply only bounded, safe, auditable recoveries automatically; and expose durable, actionable state so users and other surfaces can understand what happened and retry. It must never freeze the UI, must not disguise provider/auth failures, must avoid destructive or unbounded repair, and must preserve the rule that an interrupted Goal enters error/failed state and can be restarted later. Provide settings and lifecycle behavior that avoid duplicate supervisor agents across restarts, make recovery idempotent, and stop or idle cleanly when no workflow work remains.

Add a persistent Supervisor tab to the existing web toolbar. The tab must remain discoverable even while the supervisor agent is idle and show live observations, current health/status, decisions, recovery attempts, outcomes, and actionable failures. It must reconnect safely after UI or daemon restart and must not depend on a Goal detail page being open. Implement the capability in shared backend services and durable state so UI, CLI, and API expose the same supervisor truth; keep the toolbar as a thin adapter.

Include proportional unit and integration coverage for active-work lifecycle, worker loss/restart, stalled work, safe recovery, unrecoverable failure, daemon restart/reconnect, concurrent Goals, duplicate-supervisor prevention, no-work idle behavior, toolbar persistence/live updates, and shared CLI/API/UI state, plus operator/user documentation and an intent/architecture update explaining the recovery boundary and relationship between the supervisor process and supervisor agent.

Make the persistent Supervisor toolbar tab interactive. The supervisor agent must accept user prompts and follow-up steering in the same way this Codex interface does. Reuse Refine’s existing chat session, message queueing, streaming output, reconnection, transcript, cancellation, and provider-error UI/backend capabilities instead of creating a separate conversation stack. The tab should combine conversational messages with structured supervisor health and recovery events without confusing the two, preserve the supervisor session across navigation and UI reconnects, allow prompts while workflows are active or idle, and clearly show when a request is queued, running, completed, failed, or blocked on provider/auth access. User prompts may guide investigation and bounded recovery but must not silently expand destructive authority; existing confirmation and audit rules still apply. Add tests for initial prompts, follow-ups during active work, queued input, reconnect/transcript restoration, provider failure, idle prompting, and coexistence with normal standalone and Goal chat tabs.

ROUND 3 ARCHITECTURE CORRECTION (authoritative): The supervisor agent itself must simply be an ordinary Refine CLI agent, using the same configured `agent_cli` provider path and the same process supervision, auth, timeouts, cancellation, concurrency accounting, streaming, transcript, queued follow-ups, provider-session resume, and failure handling as the rest of the system. Refine may coordinate its lifecycle and provide structured context, but do not create a second custom reasoning or fixing engine and do not duplicate capabilities that the existing workflow agents, chat service, process supervisor, WorkflowEngine, Git sync service, projection, or activity/event services already provide.

When queued or active Goal-agent work appears, automatically start or resume exactly one supervisor CLI-agent turn for that active-work window (subject to the existing provider/global concurrency limits). Give that CLI agent a supervisor prompt and existing Refine CLI/API tools so it can watch the active work until the queue becomes idle, inspect the shared workflow/process/Git evidence, apply bounded safe in-scope fixes, and report anything that needs user authority. New actionable events should steer or queue follow-up context into that same session, and user prompts from the persistent Supervisor toolbar tab must use the same session. The supervisor must idle/exit cleanly when no work remains, restart safely after interruption, and never duplicate itself.

Use existing backend truth for signals: Goal/workflow transitions and retry state from WorkflowEngine/work items, agent/process state and output from the process supervisor/provider records, Git/state sync results from the Git sync service, and existing operations/activity evidence. A small durable supervisor snapshot/event projection is acceptable for cross-surface UI/CLI/API visibility and deduplication, but it must be an adapter/coordinator, not a parallel rules engine. Do not infer a stall from the Goal `updated` timestamp alone while a live agent process is producing output. Do not rewrite the durable snapshot on read or on a no-op reconcile, because that would create continuous `refine/state` sync churn.

Review round-2 candidate `3a6d59d0c1483274d885dcb1d71bfc91231018f5` and selectively reuse its proven Supervisor chat attachment, persistent toolbar UI, shared API/CLI view, documentation, and no-op-write protection. Reconcile it with current `main`, including the already-merged live Goal-log toolbar; do not overwrite either feature. Remove or replace any round-2 logic that duplicates existing agent/workflow/process/Git capabilities or leaves the CLI agent prompt-only rather than automatically supervising active work.

Add executable integration evidence that the configured CLI provider is actually launched through the existing provider/process path when work becomes active; only one supervisor is launched across concurrent Goals and daemon reconnects; user and system follow-ups share its queue/transcript; live process progress prevents false stalls; a genuinely lost/stalled worker creates actionable context; bounded recovery and unrecoverable provider/auth failures are visible; no-work idle stops further turns; and repeated reads/no-op reconciliation do not rewrite durable state or trigger Git-sync churn. Keep all current broad tests, strict Clippy, docs, CLI reference, and browser checks green.

failed_actions.message: Existing durable supervisor sessions are reused without verifying their provider against the configured agent_cli. Dispatch uses the session provider while capacity accounting uses the configured provider, leaving two inconsistent provider truths and preserving pre-round-4 overrides.
failed_actions.rule: Each concern should have one authoritative implementation and source of truth.

failed_actions.message: Stopping an active Supervisor conversation only marks the chat session closed; it does not signal the supervised provider process. The process and capacity lease remain active until the provider exits naturally, so the toolbar's Stop action is not effective cancellation.
failed_actions.rule: Long-running agent work requires supervised cancellation and recovery.

failed_actions.message: Tests cover fresh configured sessions and naturally completed provider turns, but not migration or reuse of an overridden durable session, provider reconfiguration, or stopping an in-flight supervisor and proving process termination and lease recovery.
failed_actions.rule: Changes crossing persistence, concurrency, and process boundaries require focused integration evidence.

rounds.implementation_report:
Ready for review at commit `480a38f`; the worktree is clean. Refine will advance the Goal after this handoff.

After-action report:

- Changed: Added shared atomic capacity leases for workflow and supervisor agents, durable capacity waiting, restart recovery, and configured-provider enforcement.
- Why: Supervisor turns previously bypassed concurrency policy and allowed provider overrides.
- Verification:
  - `cargo test` — exit 0; 380 passed, 0 failed.
  - Strict Clippy — exit 0.
  - CLI surface tests — exit 0; 1 passed.
  - JavaScript syntax/browser tests — exit 0; 2 passed.
  - Formatting, static assets, CLI reference, and Git diff checks — exit 0.
- Goal-agent usage: 271,886 tokens over approximately 19 minutes 33 seconds.
rounds.prompt:
Governance correctly rejected round 3 under rule-7 and rule-5. Fix the architectural violations without weakening Governance.

Start by preserving the complete round-3 candidate at c730b2ebc054c0329161a7114879b45b46ce61d0 (cherry-pick or otherwise carry it into this round). Then:

1. Make every supervisor CLI-agent turn participate in the existing shared agent concurrency policy: global, per-node, per-provider, and per-target-app caps. Do not create a parallel scheduler or duplicate capacity rules. Extract/reuse a shared capacity/lease primitive behind WorkflowEngine and chat/provider dispatch so Goal agents and supervisor turns see one atomic source of capacity truth.
2. At cap 1, a supervisor and Goal agent must never overlap. At cap 2, they may overlap. Queued supervisor system context and user follow-ups must remain durable and dispatch when shared capacity becomes available.
3. Release/recover supervisor capacity on success, provider/auth failure, interruption, daemon restart, stopped/closed sessions, and abandoned dispatch, without leaking a slot or spawning duplicates.
4. The Supervisor session API must use the configured agent_cli provider. Remove/reject any request-body provider override; an existing ordinary configured-provider path remains the source of truth.
5. Preserve the persistent interactive Supervisor toolbar, shared chat/provider/process/auth/streaming/evidence architecture, Goal-log toolbar, and the workflow-agent registry fix in c730b2e.
6. Add executable integration tests that exercise supervisor + WorkflowEngine together under cap 1 and cap 2, provider override behavior, durable queueing, and lease recovery. Run strict Clippy, full Rust tests, CLI surface tests, relevant JavaScript/browser tests, formatting, and docs/reference checks.

The desired architecture is a supervisor role policy over Refine's existing agent substrate, not a second agent executor, scheduler, workflow engine, process manager, Git engine, or recovery engine.

failed_actions.message: The persistent Supervisor toolbar adds polling, session restoration, interactive chat, and live health rendering, but its only UI-specific test checks source-code substrings. The executable browser tests still cover only Goal logs, so Supervisor persistence, reconnect, live updates, queued interaction, and failure rendering are not behaviorally verified.
failed_actions.rule: Require evidence proportional to architectural and operational risk. Changes crossing capabilities, surfaces, persistence, concurrency, process, Git, release, or recovery boundaries need focused integration evidence, visible failure behavior, and affected intent documentation.

rounds.implementation_report:
Goal `00000SZ1T921F15X91MBZWE000` is ready for review at commit `254b55b85896f71e09b17b0835c797802fcc4fdc`. Worktree is clean.

After-action report:

- Changed: Unified Supervisor and Goal provider process registration, enforced configured-provider migration, added real managed-process Stop/cancellation, preserved capacity until process exit, and added lifecycle tests/docs.
- Why: Supervisor sessions previously could use stale providers, Stop did not terminate providers, and workflow cancellation searched the wrong process registry.
- Verification:
